extra.reasoning:
The property is monotonicity in pallor: setting pallor=true (all else unchanged) must never lower acuity. Pallor appears in only one rule, interaction_drop (priority 100), which requires all 12 of severe_headache, palpitations, rash, fever, back_pain, dizziness, syncope, abdominal_pain, confusion, focal_weakness, vision_loss, pallor to be true and forces acuity := self_care (lowest). Its priority 100 dominates every other rule (max other priority is 3).

Counterexample comparison: take all of the 12 non-pallor conjunction symptoms true (plus chest_pain true, irrelevant).
- Baseline (pallor=false): interaction_drop does NOT fire because pallor is missing. Multiple emergent rules fire (raise_palpitations, raise_focal_weakness, raise_dizziness, raise_fever, raise_abdominal_pain, all priority 3) -> highest matching priority 3 -> acuity = emergent.
- After flipping pallor=true (the witness, all 13 inputs true): the full conjunction is satisfied, so interaction_drop (priority 100) fires and wins -> acuity = self_care.

Acuity drops from emergent (highest) to self_care (lowest) purely by setting pallor true. This lowers acuity, violating the monotonicity property. The reported witness_assignment is the violating input (pallor=true); its pallor=false counterpart yields the strictly higher acuity emergent.
